texto: The lesson of the Cuban Missile Crisis is plain: Strength prevents war weakness invites it. We need a commander-in-chief who understands that - and who won't leave us facing a foe who thinks he doesn't.

frase_original: The lesson of the Cuban Missile Crisis is plain: Strength prevents war weakness invites it. We need a commander-in-chief who understands that - and who won't leave us facing a foe who thinks he doesn't.

texto: Millions of Americans cannot tell you who lived at Mount Vernon or who wrote the Declaration of Independence - let alone the Emancipation Proclamation. But they know that to be 'a Benedict Arnold' is to be a traitor of the deepest dye - someone who coldly betrays not only a sacred cause but every moral scruple along the way.
explicacao: Citação de Arthur L. Herman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Millions of Americans cannot tell you who lived at Mount Vernon or who wrote the Declaration of I…” articula vida, alone, moral em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como termino e moral.
